body.title: [sprint] DS live-smoke pytest: drive _run_inference(hello) against real devstral@Hex, assert DONE→close
body.intention: I intend that the first proven devstral build is a repeatable artifact, so the keystone (does devstral emit native tool_calls and reach DONE?) is verifiable on demand, not a one-off.
body.description:
**Problem:** The existing dicksimnel tests (test_end_to_end.py, simulator.py, simulator_test.py) do NOT drive live inference — they test the Critic on synthetic data and REPLAY closed tickets. Nothing exercises a real devstral dispatch→ToolLoop→DONE. The Jun-11 run (old OpenRouter/opus config) hit max-turns(20)→escalation; the current devstral/ollama path has NEVER reached DONE in an agentic loop. THE KEYSTONE RISK.
**Proposed shape:** A pytest (live, network-gated/skipped when Hex unreachable) that builds the trivial hello-ticket dict, instantiates `DickSimnelDevice()`, calls `dev._run_inference(ticket)` (device.py:468 — smallest live path, bypasses the bus), and asserts the return is a DONE envelope (`_parse_terminal_response`) and `_post_result` closes it. Run against REAL `devstral-small-2:24b` on Hex (requires T-ds-local-ollama-route routing + the model pulled). Mark `@pytest.mark.live`/skip-if-unreachable so CI without Hex stays green.
**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/dicksimnel/ (new live smoke test); reuse ToolLoop/_run_inference/_post_result as-is.
**Design rules:** Inference Proxy only; log dispatch_started/tool-calls/DONE at the crossings; no SQLite; the test must be skip-soft when Hex is down (no false red in CI).
**Scope boundary:** the bypass `_run_inference` path only; the FULL Granny+bus dispatch path is the operational run (not this ticket).
**Completion criteria:** with Hex up + devstral pulled + routing wired, the pytest drives a real devstral ToolLoop that emits native tool_calls, executes the Write, returns DONE, and closes the ticket — green. With Hex down it skips, not fails.
**Test plan:** the pytest itself is the proof; assert DONE envelope + ticket closed + SMOKE_OK.txt content; assert skip path when INFERENCE_ENDPOINT host is unreachable.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
